scope.target: GitHub Actions trigger, lifecycle, concurrency, permission, artifact, and mutation contracts
constraints: Keep release mutation behind the owned tag workflow
Do not claim hosted evidence from local YAML
non_goals: Publish or rehearse a release without owner authorization

steps: Implement the approved version-scoped phase contract
execution.last_completed_step: PR #7 passed the accepted governance, portable, and candidate-discovery boundaries; candidate and tag action execution transferred to the governed release lifecycle
execution.next_action: Completed; the v0.6.0 release-publication workflow owns remaining candidate, tag, publication, and finalization gates

results.summary: Implemented and tested the CI-001/CI-002 four-workflow lifecycle, Node.js 24-native artifact actions, concurrency, fail-closed ownership, and version-scoped phase contracts. PR #7 supplied hosted trigger evidence; actual candidate upload and tag publication transfer remain mandatory release-phase gates.
tests_run: Shell asset and AI-context validators: passed
Quick aggregate gate: 44 required checks passed, 0 failed, 2 not applicable
results.residual_risk: The candidate PR and tag-triggered publication must still execute the selected artifact actions successfully; the release phase contract fails closed if either execution is absent or unsuccessful.
